zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh Calverly於1355年7月12日出生在英國爾莫特拉姆 聖 安德魯時，他的父親David De Calveley30歲，母親Agnes de Mottram28歲。他於1381年與Agnes Anne Hawberk在英國爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1393年4月12日在他的家鄉享年37歲。

nameMeanings.name: Calverly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Calverley
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from Calverley in Yorkshire, named with Old English calfra (genitive plural of calf ‘calf’) + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Alternatively, in some instances the name may be a variant of the habitational name Calveley, from a place so called in Cheshire (with the same meaning as above), or from Calverleigh in Devon, meaning ‘the clearing of the bare wood’ (Old English calu ‘bare, bald’ + wudu ‘wood’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh Calverly was born on 12 July 1355, in Mottram St Andrew, Cheshire, England, his father, David De Calveley, was 30 and his mother, Agnes de Mottram, was 28. He married Agnes Anne Hawberk in 1381, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 12 April 1393, in his hometown, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh Calverly est né le 12 juillet 1355, à Mottram St Andrew, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, David De Calveley, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Mottram, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Anne Hawberk en 1381, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 12 avril 1393, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh Calverly am 12. Juli 1355 in Mottram St Andrew, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater David De Calveley 30 und seine Mutter Agnes de Mottram 28. Er hat 1381 in Cheshire, England Agnes Anne Hawberk geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 12. April 1393 im Alter von 37 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh Calverly è nato il il 12 luglio 1355 nel Mottram St Andrew, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, David De Calveley, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de Mottram, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Anne Hawberk nel 1381, nel Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 12 aprile 1393, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 37 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Calverly(이)가 1355년 7월 12일 영국 체실르 모트램 스트 앤드루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David De Calveley(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Mottram(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1381년 영국 체실르에서 Agnes Anne Hawberk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1393년 4월 12일 그의 고향에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.
